title: Acute Appendicitis with Perforation
chief_complaint: Abdominal pain started around my belly button yesterday, now it's moved to my right lower side. It's constant and getting worse. I have no appetite, I vomited twice, and I feel feverish.

physical_exam.Abdomen: RLQ tenderness with guarding and rebound tenderness. McBurney point tenderness. Rovsing sign positive. Psoas sign positive (retrocecal appendix). Obturator sign negative.
physical_exam.Labs: WBC 17,500 with 88% neutrophils, CRP 150. CT: Dilated appendix 12mm with wall thickening, periappendiceal fat stranding, small periappendiceal abscess 2cm, free fluid in pelvis. No free air (no frank perforation).

correct_diagnosis: Acute Appendicitis with Periappendiceal Abscess (Complicated Appendicitis)
key_learning_points: Classic presentation: Periumbilical pain → migrates to RLQ + anorexia + nausea/vomiting + low-grade fever. Atypical presentations common in elderly, pregnant, children, retrocecal appendix
Alvarado score ≥7 = high risk. CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast is diagnostic gold standard (sensitivity 95%). Ultrasound first-line in children and pregnancy

Complicated appendicitis (perforation, abscess): Up to 30% present with perforation. Management: IV antibiotics (Piperacillin-Tazobactam or Ceftriaxone + Metronidazole) + Percutaneous drainage of abscess >3cm + Interval appendectomy 6-12 weeks later. Uncomplicated: Early laparoscopic appendectomy within 24h
Non-operative management with antibiotics alone: 70% success at 1 year. 25-40% recurrence at 5 years. Appropriate for selected patients without appendicolith

Surgery: Laparoscopic (preferred): Less pain, shorter stay, faster recovery, fewer wound infections. Open if laparoscopy contraindicated. Single-dose pre-op antibiotics (Cefoxitin or Cefazolin + Metronidazole). Post-op: Advance diet, early ambulation, D/C 24-48h uncomplicated

hidden_data.cbc: WBC: 12,500 /µL | RBC: 4.5 M/µL | Hb: 13.8 g/dL | Hct: 41% | MCV: 88 fL | Platelets: 250,000 /µL
hidden_data.cmp: Na: 140 mmol/L | K: 4.0 mmol/L | Cl: 103 mmol/L | HCO3: 24 mmol/L | BUN: 15 mg/dL | Cr: 1.0 mg/dL | Glucose: 100 mg/dL | Ca: 9.5 mg/dL | Alb: 4.2 g/dL | AST: 28 U/L | ALT: 32 U/L | ALP: 75 U/L | T.Bil: 0.7 mg/dL
hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Dilated appendix 12mm with wall thickening and periappendiceal fat stranding
